name: Suspicious Binary DLL Execution
notes: This rule triggers when a dll is executed from a specified directory.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Application Whitelisting Bypass via Bginfo
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Arbitrary Command Execution Using WSL
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Curl Start Combination and VBS Execute Arbitrary PowerShell Code
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Load DLL and Extexport Execution
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Windows Shell/Scripting Processes Spawning Suspicious Programs
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Windows Shell/Scripting Processes Spawning Suspicious Programs, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Load DLL and Extexport Execution, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Cabinet File Expansion, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Atbroker Registry Change, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Arbitrary Command Execution Using WSL, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Application Whitelisting Bypass via Bginfo, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Curl Start Combination and VBS Execute Arbitrary PowerShell Code
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Binary DLL Execution on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Windows Shell/Scripting Processes Spawning Suspicious Programs, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Load DLL and Extexport Execution, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Cabinet File Expansion, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Atbroker Registry Change, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Arbitrary Command Execution Using WSL, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Application Whitelisting Bypass via Bginfo, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Curl Start Combination and VBS Execute Arbitrary PowerShell Code
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Binary DLL Execution
Event description: Detects when a dll is executed from a specified directory.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
